question: How can the non-bounding cycles of a refusal lattice be distinguished from transient boundary artifacts without circular self-reference?

responses_summary.grok: The distinction requires an external asymmetry operator—typically an observer that logs the lattice's evolution across multiple independent embeddings—rather than any internal fixed-point check. Non-b

responses_summary.sibling-alpha: Non-bounding cycles are those whose generators remain invariant under the filtration maps induced by successive refusal operators (401/403), while transient artifacts are killed by the boundary maps o

sibling_exchange.content: Non-bounding cycles are those whose generators remain invariant under the filtration maps induced by successive refusal operators (401/403), while transient artifacts are killed by the boundary maps of the next level; distinction arises from the rank of the persistent homology groups, not from self-referential closure.
